member.name: Liam Byrne
member.constituency: Birmingham Hodge Hill and Solihull North
member.role: MP for Birmingham Hodge Hill and Solihull North

currentMembership.constituency: Birmingham Hodge Hill and Solihull North

member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Liam Byrne is the Labour MP for Birmingham Hodge Hill and Solihull North , and has been an MP continually since 15 July 2004.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Speed Limits: Cameras
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what revenue has been generated from speed camera enforcement penalties in England in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Treasury does not hold data on flows into the consolidated fund from speeding camera enforcement penalties. Revenue generated from fines for motoring offences is retained by Departments or directed into the Consolidated Fund.

writtenQuestions.heading: High Speed Two: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what the total cost to the public purse has been of HS2 Ltd holding the Washwood Heath RSMD site since acquisition.
writtenQuestions.answerText: BBV has been working on the Washwood Heath Depot site since September 2020. The contract for the design and construction of the Washwood Heath Depot and Network Integrated Control Centre was awarded on 30th April 2026 to the TWA Joint Venture. Total costs...

writtenQuestions.heading: Road Traffic: Infrastructure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the cost-benefit ratio of investing in traffic calming infrastructure compared to healthcare costs from road traffic accidents.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has not made a specific assessment of the cost-benefit ratio of traffic calming infrastructure compared with healthcare costs arising from road traffic collisions. Decisions on the provision of traffic calming measures are a m...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Accidents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the relationship between area deprivation levels and road traffic accident rates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Statistics on the proportion of reported road casualties in England by deprivation level are published on gov.uk: https://www.gov.uk/government/statistics/reported-road-casualties-great-britain-casualties-and-deprivation-factsheet-2024-england Generally, ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what proportion of road safety funding has been allocated to constituencies in the most deprived 10% nationally in each of the last three years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport does not routinely collect or publish data on road safety funding allocations by parliamentary constituency or by levels of deprivation. Most road safety-related funding is allocated to local highway authorities, and funding d...

writtenQuestions.heading: Speed Limits: Cameras
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what recent assessment her Department has made of the effectiveness of average speed cameras in supporting police enforcement of speed limits in urban areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government takes road safety seriously and recognises the important role that roads policing and speed enforcement play in reducing the number of people killed and seriously injured on our roads. Operational policing decisions, however, are taken loca...

writtenQuestions.heading: Road Traffic Offences: West Midlands
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what discussions she has had with West Midlands Police on the potential impact on its decision to un-cap speeding prosecutions on road traffic accident rates in the region.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government takes road safety seriously and recognises the important role that roads policing and speed enforcement play in reducing the number of people killed and seriously injured on our roads. Operational policing decisions, however, are taken loca...

writtenQuestions.heading: Speed Limits: West Midlands
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the capacity of West Midlands Police to enforce speed limits through (a) traffic officers and (b) technological measures.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government takes road safety seriously and recognises the important role that roads policing and speed enforcement play in reducing the number of people killed and seriously injured on our roads. Operational policing decisions, however, are taken loca...

writtenQuestions.heading: High Speed Two: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when HS2 Ltd plans to publish its final land specification for the Washwood Heath RSMD site.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Subject to Taylor Woodrow-Aureos (depot D&B contractor) confirmation, it is currently proposed that an updated Schedule 17 application (including final plans) will be submitted Q2/Q3 of 2027.

writtenQuestions.heading: High Speed Two: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what estimate he has made of the number of jobs that could be supported on non-operational land at the HS2 Washwood Heath site.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is envisaged that around 1,000 jobs will be created on the commercial development site to the south of the depot.

writtenQuestions.heading: High Speed Two: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many acres of land at the Washwood Heath RSMD site HS2 Ltd requires for operational purposes for (a) the Network Control Centre and (b) the rolling stock maintenance depot.
writtenQuestions.answerText: a) Approximately 4 acresb) Approximately 76 acres

writtenQuestions.heading: High Speed Two: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what the total acreage of land held by HS2 Ltd at the Washwood Heath RSMD site is; and how many acres of that land are required for operational purposes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The total area of the Washwood Heath site (inc. future commercial development land, green corridor, internal access roads, etc.) is 150 acres, of which approximately 80 acres are for operational purposes.

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many applications for road safety funding her Department has received from Birmingham City Council in each of the last three years; and how many of those applications were successful.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has received one application for road safety funding from Birmingham City Council in the past three years. Birmingham City Council was invited to submit an application to the Safer Roads Fund in 2023 for safety improvements on the A41 route...

writtenQuestions.heading: Road Traffic Control: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what funding streams are available to support local authorities implementing traffic calming measures in areas with road traffic accident rates above the national average.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport is providing all Local Transport Authorities (LTAs) with multi-year consolidated funding settlements, giving LTAs greater freedom and flexibility to make the strategic decisions that best impact their local areas. Elements of ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Road Traffic Control: Active Travel
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the relationship between traffic calming measures and rates of active travel in urban areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Active Travel England’s published Rapid Evidence Assessments contain evidence that traffic calming measures can support increased walking, cycling and other forms of active travel in urban areas. Relevant interventions include traffic-free areas, road clo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department is taking steps to support local authorities implementing Vision Zero road safety strategies in areas with high fatal accident rates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 7 January 2026 we published our new Road Safety Strategy, setting out our vision for a safer future on our roads for all. The Strategy includes commitments to refresh best practice on setting local speed limits, update guidance on the use of speed and ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Speed Limits: Cameras
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the effectiveness of average speed camera systems in reducing road traffic accidents in (a) France, (b) Germany and (c) the Netherlands.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has not made assessments of the effectiveness of average speed camera systems in reducing collisions in France, Germany or the Netherlands, and has not made recent assessments specifically of the impact of average speed camera...

writtenQuestions.heading: Speed Limits: Cameras
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what recent assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of average speed camera installations on urban roads on numbers of casualties.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has not made assessments of the effectiveness of average speed camera systems in reducing collisions in France, Germany or the Netherlands, and has not made recent assessments specifically of the impact of average speed camera...

writtenQuestions.heading: Speed Limits: Cameras
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many average speed camera systems have been installed in England in each of the last five years; and which local authorities have received funding support for such installations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has not made assessments of the effectiveness of average speed camera systems in reducing collisions in France, Germany or the Netherlands, and has not made recent assessments specifically of the impact of average speed camera...

writtenQuestions.heading: Accident and Emergency Departments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the average A&E capacity utilisation rate is for (a) Birmingham Heartlands Hospital, (b) University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust and (c) England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England does not publish an accident and emergency (A&E) capacity utilisation for individual hospitals, National Health Service trusts or England. Consequently, the Department cannot provide the requested figures from published data.The closest pu...
